blurb: Helsing is a European defence-AI company building software and increasingly hardware (autonomous aircraft, strike drones, robotics) for military applications, with a strong 'European sovereignty' framing.

news.title: Helsing Introduces the CA-1 Electronic Attack, an Autonomous Combat Aircraft for Electronic Warfare
news.summary: Helsing (Munich) unveils the CA-1, an autonomous combat aircraft for electronic warfare — a notable escalation from defence software into autonomous lethal hardware, directly relevant to lethal-autonomous-weapons (LAWS) policy debates.

news.title: Helsing and EURENCO sign partnership to deliver a sovereign European strike drone capability
news.summary: Partnership with EURENCO to build a 'sovereign European' strike-drone capability — ties autonomous-weapons development to European strategic-autonomy / defence-sovereignty policy.

notes: Harvested 2026-06-27 via OpenClaw headless browser (helsing.ai/newsroom passed cleanly; untested by web_fetch this morning). Dates confirmed from each article's own 'Press Release DD. Mon. YYYY' header. Selected the two items with the strongest AI-policy signal (autonomous combat aircraft; sovereign European strike drones) as defence-AI / LAWS / European-sovereignty signals. Other dated newsroom items exist (OHB/Helsing space JVs 'KIRK'/'Alliance for Europe's Space Sector', RX-1 robotics platform, Stockholm office, CCO/CFO appointments) but are corporate/personnel rather than policy — left as leads, not logged, to keep signal high. Newsroom listing links wrapped images with no inline text on re-query, so dates were read from individual article headers rather than the index.
